prediction.summary: An insider threat incident involving user emma.hr has been detected. The user, who recently submitted their resignation, accessed multiple sensitive HR and legal documents, performed a bulk download of 842MB, copied the data to a removable USB device, and initiated a connection to a personal cloud sync service. This activity triggered a DLP policy violation and was flagged by the SIEM as a high-severity insider threat pattern.

timeline.event: User emma.hr performed a bulk download of 842MB archive /tmp/eh_export.zip
User emma.hr copied 842MB to a removable device E:\backup
User emma.hr connected a personal cloud sync client to sync.evil-drop.io
DLP policy violation: HR confidential data staged for external sync by emma.hr
SIEM detected insider threat pattern: bulk sensitive file access + USB + cloud sync
SIEM noted user emma.hr's resignation submitted 48 hours ago, elevating risk score
SIEM identified MITRE T1530 Data from Cloud Storage / internal collection
SIEM issued HIGH severity alert for insider_threat incident involving user emma.hr
HR audit reported emma.hr accessed 47 files outside normal role scope in 54 minutes

recommendations: Immediately suspend all network and system access for user emma.hr.
Initiate a forensic investigation of LAPTOP-HR-09 to determine the full scope of data copied to the USB device and any other exfiltration attempts.
Review emma.hr's access logs and activity history for the past several weeks/months to identify any other unusual data access or exfiltration attempts.
Engage HR and Legal teams regarding potential data theft and legal implications.
Review and enhance DLP policies and controls, especially for departing employees, to prevent similar incidents.
